techniques.evidence_quote: an intrusion into Code Spaces' Amazon EC2 control panel
The beginning of the end was a DDoS attack initiated yesterday
he had removed all EBS snapshots, S3 buckets, all AMI's, some EBS instances and several machine instances
Extortion demands were left for Code Spaces officials
all of its svn repositories—backups and snapshots—were deleted

attack_chain.description: Attacker launched a DDoS attack against Code Spaces as the opening move of the intrusion.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The beginning of the end was a DDoS attack initiated yesterday

evidence_chunks.quote: The beginning of the end was a DDoS attack initiated yesterday
The beginning of the end was a DDoS attack initiated yesterday

attack_chain.description: Attacker gained unauthorized access to the Amazon EC2 control panel using valid AWS console credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: accompanied by an intrusion into Code Spaces' Amazon EC2 control panel

attack_chain.description: Attacker left extortion demands along with a Hotmail contact address demanding payment from Code Spaces.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Extortion demands were left for Code Spaces officials, along with a Hotmail address they were supposed to use to contact the attackers

evidence_chunks.quote: Extortion demands were left for Code Spaces officials, along with a Hotmail address they were supposed to use to contact the attackers
Extortion demands were left for Code Spaces officials, along with a Hotmail address they were supposed to use to contact the attackers

attack_chain.description: Attacker created additional backup logins within the AWS console to maintain persistence after the legitimate owners attempted to lock them out.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker had created backup logins

evidence_chunks.quote: the attacker had created backup logins
the attacker had created backup logins

attack_chain.description: Once Code Spaces began recovery actions, the attacker deleted EBS snapshots, S3 buckets, all AMIs, some EBS instances/volumes, and several EC2 instances.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: he had removed all EBS snapshots, S3 buckets, all AMI's, some EBS instances and several machine instances

attack_chain.description: Attacker specifically destroyed backups, snapshots, and offsite backups, preventing Code Spaces from recovering and forcing it out of business.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: all of its svn repositories—backups and snapshots—were deleted. All EBS volumes containing database files were also deleted

evidence_chunks.quote: all of its svn repositories—backups and snapshots—were deleted. All EBS volumes containing database files were also deleted
all of its svn repositories—backups and snapshots—were deleted. All EBS volumes containing database files were also deleted

initial_access_vector: Attacker gained access to Code Spaces' AWS EC2 control panel using valid console credentials (suspected phishing), coinciding with a DDoS attack.
persistence_mechanism: Attacker created additional backup logins (cloud accounts) in the AWS control panel to retain access after password resets.
privilege_escalation: Not explicitly described; initial AWS console credentials already had administrative privileges sufficient for destructive actions.
